name: Button
summary: HTML+CSS button. Variant axes match the Figma Components page exactly: configuration (linkbutton|text|outlined|filled|tonal) × icon (no-icon|with-icon-left|with-icon-right) × size (Small|Medium|Large) × color (primary). A `filled` button is the single CTA per section; supporting actions use `outlined`/`text`/`linkbutton`.

anatomy.description: Poppins SemiBold; UPPERCASE per the Button type style.

props.name: configuration
props.description: Visual style; not an HTML attribute — emit as CSS modifier class.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Sets `disabled` attribute; never replace with `aria-disabled` alone.

states.name: enabled
states.description: Resting; filled = Primary 500 bg, white label.

states.name: hovered
states.description: filled → Primary 600 bg; outlined → Primary 50 bg fill.

states.name: disabled
states.description: Disabled Text `#A19F9D` + muted fill; never lower whole-element opacity.

states.name: focus-visible
states.description: Always include a visible focus ring — never `outline: none` without a replacement.

a11y: Always use a real `<button>`; never an `<a>` with a JS click handler for an action.
`linkbutton` looks like a link but IS a button — `<button class="glds-btn glds-btn--linkbutton">`.
Material icon-only controls (rare in this set) MUST carry `aria-label`.
Keep `:focus-visible` ring; tap targets ≥ 48px on mobile.

examples.label: Outlined with leading icon

doNot: Use an `<a>` with a JS handler for an action — use `linkbutton`.
Use a `<button>` for plain navigation — use `<a href>`.
Place two `filled` buttons next to each other (competing primaries).
Hide the focus ring without a visible replacement.
Use vague labels like 'Submit' or 'Click here' — write action+value (`Enroll now`).
